globalOptions.name: --version
globalOptions.description: Output the jq version and exit with zero.

globalOptions.name: --stream
globalOptions.description: Parse the input in streaming fashion, outputting arrays of path and leaf values.

globalOptions.name: --slurp
globalOptions.description: Read (slurp) all inputs into an array; apply filter to it.

globalOptions.name: --raw-input
globalOptions.description: Don't parse the input as JSON.

globalOptions.name: --null-input
globalOptions.description: Don't read any input at all! Instead, the filter is run once using `null` as the input.

globalOptions.name: --compact-output
globalOptions.description: By default, jq pretty-prints JSON output. Using this option will result in more compact output.

globalOptions.name: --tab
globalOptions.description: Use a tab for each indentation level instead of two spaces.

globalOptions.name: --color-output
globalOptions.description: Output in color even if writing to a pipe or file.

globalOptions.name: --sort-keys
globalOptions.description: Output the fields of each object with the keys in sorted order.

globalOptions.name: --raw-output
globalOptions.description: Output raw strings, not JSON texts.

globalOptions.name: --arg
globalOptions.description: This option passes a value to the jq program as a predefined variable.
